figures.caption: Fig. 2. Qualitative comparison between ground truth and predicted heatmaps for three tool–action pairs: (i) hook - dissection, (ii) clipper - clipping, and (iii) scissors -clipping, across six representative timestamps.

Fig. 1. Architecture of AffordTissue: a) High-level overview: The pipeline processes tool-action prompts and temporal vision context via frozen SigLIP and Swin encoders, with a trained AdaLN-based decoder fusing these embeddings to output a heatmap. b) Decoder details: The decoder utilizes text-conditioned adaptive layer normalization (AdaLN) to refine temporal vision latents for a specific tool-action pair. This results in pixel-level logits that estimate the probability of each pixel belonging to the safe tissue affordance region.
